page: UpCloud 8xCPU-32GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core, alongside 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 640 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores reach average levels. Memory bandwidth is highly dependent on block size; small-block cached reads perform at average speeds, but large-block uncached writes are poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Static web serving throughput is also weak, registering in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that can leverage multi-threading to offset the lower single-core performance. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation. However, it is not recommended for memory-intensive large-block write operations or medium-to-large LLM inference tasks due to significant performance limitations.
description: UpCloud 8xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor featuring 8 shared vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 CPU-to-memory ratio. The instance includes a bundled 640 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities, standard software compilation speeds, and average Redis SET operations, though single-core performance and large-block memory write bandwidth are weak. This resource profile makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and Redis caching. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications, high-throughput static web serving, or large-scale LLM inference tasks.
og_description: UpCloud 8xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 640 GB local SSD. It offers average multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and bundled local SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
32.0 GB system memory and 640 GB bundled local SSD storage
Average multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
Weak single-core performance and large-block memory write bandwidth
Suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded tasks
